jobs.title: IT Product Manager Intern, Assets
jobs.content_plain: About Us At Cloudflare, we are on a mission to help build a better Internet. Today the company runs one of the world’s largest networks that powers millions of websites and other Internet properties for customers ranging from individual bloggers to SMBs to Fortune 500 companies. Cloudflare protects and accelerates any Internet application online without adding hardware, installing software, or changing a line of code. Internet properties powered by Cloudflare all have web traffic routed through its intelligent global network, which gets smarter with every request. As a result, they see significant improvement in performance and a decrease in spam and other attacks. Cloudflare was named to Entrepreneur Magazine’s Top Company Cultures list and ranked among the World’s Most Innovative Companies by Fast Company.  At Cloudflare, we’re not looking for people who wait for a polished roadmap; we’re looking for the builders who see the cracks in the Internet that everyone else has simply learned to live with. We value candidates who have the instinct to spot a "normalized" problem and the AI-native curiosity to create a solution using the latest tools. Our culture is built on iteration, leveraging AI to ship faster today to make it better tomorrow, while ensuring that every improvement, no matter how small, is shared across the team to lift everyone up. If you’re the type of person who values curiosity over bureaucracy, and that AI is a partner in solving tough problems to keep the Internet moving forward, you’ll fit right in. Available Location: Austin Role Summary:  As an IT Product Manager Intern, you will play a pivotal role in transforming how Cloudflare’s internal teams triage, prioritize, and execute work. You will be on a mission to break down silos and supercharge how internal teams collaborate. This role focuses on redefining processes by leveraging AI to generate dynamic assets and automate workflows. We are looking for an analytical, innovative, and empathetic thinker who is passionate about leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) and Generative AI to solve complex workflow inefficiencies, streamline communication, and ensure strong alignment between business and technical teams.  A variety of IT Product Management focal areas include: Strategic Asset Automation - you will redefine how product documentation drives IT development at Cloudflare. Instead of manually drafting static documents, you will leverage AI to generate dynamic, high-fidelity assets—from Business Requirement Documents (BRDs), user stories, acceptance criteria to technical vendor analyses.  Collaboration Acceleration -  you will be on a mission to break down silos and supercharge how our internal teams work together and continually align. You will act as a researcher and strategist, conducting deep-dive interviews with Product Managers and stakeholders across the organization to uncover hidden friction points in communication and cross-functional alignment and progress updates.  Intake Strategy & Automation - you will play a pivotal role in transforming how Cloudflare’s internal teams triage, prioritize, and execute work. You will partner with engineering teams and internal stakeholders to design and implement an AI-driven solution that automates ticket intake, significantly reducing manual triage overhead.  Note: The projects listed above are examples of the high-impact work we focus on. None of these projects are guaranteed. The actual internship project(s) will be assigned based on Cloudflare's most critical business priorities at the time of the summer 2026 internship. Role Responsibilities: Lead the product discovery and definition for internal systems, translating high-level business needs into clear technical specifications and user stories. Lead comprehensive user research by interviewing internal stakeholders to map out the current collaboration landscape and identify major pain points. Architect AI-driven document pipelines that ingest raw stakeholder inputs and output structured, standard-compliant assets such as Business Requirement Documents (BRDs), Product Roadmaps, and User Personas. Design and validate AI-driven logic workflows and solutions—such as automated meeting synthesis, intelligent knowledge retrieval, or cross-team dependency alerts—that automatically identify and cluster similar tickets, enabling teams to manage "themes" of work rather than individual tasks. Collaborate with engineering teams to implement thoughtful automation and deliver a functional MVP (Minimum Viable Product) that integrates seamlessly into Cloudflare’s existing tool stack. Innovate using an AI strategy to maintain the product backlog for internal platforms, helping to make trade-off decisions between speed of delivery and feature depth based on stakeholder inputs and business value. Optimize the "Discovery to Development" velocity by identifying friction points in the documentation process and deploying generative solutions that reduce lag time. Develop validation frameworks that utilize AI to cross-reference incoming requests against existing requests and OKRs, ensuring resources are automatically prioritized for high-impact initiatives. Define and deploy success metrics for improvements, measuring how your solutions reduce manual triage overhead, meeting fatigue, email overhead, or information search time. Must-Have Skills: Educational Background: Currently enrolled in or recently graduated from a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program in Computer Science, Business Administration, Information Systems, Organizational Psychology, Engineering Management, or a related field. AI/ML Fluency: A strong conceptual understanding of Artificial Intelligence, specifically how LLMs and Natural Language Processing (NLP) can be applied to text analysis, workflow automation, text summarization, sentiment analysis, and knowledge management. Practical experience or strong theoretical knowledge of prompting LLMs to generate structured outputs is essential. Product Fundamentals: Demonstrated understanding of the Product Management lifecycle, including requirements gathering, writing user stories, and agile/scrum methodologies. Proficiency in defining problems, validating hypotheses, and prioritizing features based on user impact. Deep familiarity with standard product artifacts (BRDs, PRDs, Roadmaps) and an understanding of their importance in the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC). Analytical & Process Mindset: Ability to map out complex process flows and identify specific areas where human intervention can be replaced by logic or automation. Ability to break down a business problem into its technical components and evaluate software vendors or tools based on objective criteria (Analytical Rigor). A mindset for efficiency and eagerness to automate repetitive writing tasks (Process Optimization). Communication & Research: Superior written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders clearly. Strong ability to conduct empathetic customer interviews, ask probing questions to get to the root cause, and synthesize unstructured feedback into actionable insights (User Research Skills). Nice-to-Have Skills: Platform Knowledge: Hands-on experience with Jira or Salesforce or similar Enterprise Service Management tools. Experience with collaboration platforms (e.g., Miro, Google Workspace). Advanced AI Knowledge: Familiarity with the concept of Model Context Protocols (MCPs) or agentic workflows.  UX/Design: Ability to create simple wireframes or mockups (using Miro or similar) to visualize your solution concepts for stakeholders. Other Experience: Prior internship experience in Product Management, Business Analysis, or IT Operations. Financial Acumen: Basic understanding of ROI (Return on Investment) calculations to support the creation of robust Business Cases. What Makes Cloudflare Special? We’re not just a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company. We’re a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company with a soul. Fundamental to our mission to help build a better Internet is protecting the free and open Internet. Project Galileo : Since 2014, we've equipped more than 2,400 journalism and civil society organizations in 111 countries with powerful tools to defend themselves against attacks that would otherwise censor their work, technology already used by Cloudflare’s enterprise customers--at no cost. Athenian Project : In 2017, we created the Athenian Project to ensure that state and local governments have the highest level of protection and reliability for free, so that their constituents have access to election information and voter registration. Since the project, we've provided services to more than 425 local government election websites in 33 states. 1.1.1.1 : We released 1.1.1.1 to help fix the foundation of the Internet by building a faster, more secure and privacy-centric public DNS resolver. This is available publicly for everyone to use - it is the first consumer-focused service Cloudflare has ever released. Here’s the deal - we don’t store client IP addresses never, ever. We will continue to abide by our privacy commitment and ensure that no user data is sold to advertisers or used to target consumers. Sound like something you’d like to be a part of? We’d love to hear from you! This position may require access to information protected under U.S. export control laws, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations. Please note that any offer of employment may be conditioned on your authorization to receive software or technology controlled under these U.S. export laws without sponsorship for an export license. Cloudflare is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.  We are committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all people and place great value in both diversity and inclusiveness.  All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to their, or any other person's, perceived or actual race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, family care status, or any other basis protected by law. We are an AA/Veterans/Disabled Employer. Cloudflare provides reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities.  Please tell us if you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job. Examples of reasonable accommodations include, but are not limited to, changing the application process, providing documents in an alternate format, using a sign language interpreter, or using specialized equipment.  If you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job, please contact us via e-mail at hr@cloudflare.com or via mail at 101 Townsend St. San Francisco, CA 94107.

jobs.title: Content Marketing Intern (Summer 2026)
jobs.content_plain: About Us At Cloudflare, we are on a mission to help build a better Internet. Today the company runs one of the world’s largest networks that powers millions of websites and other Internet properties for customers ranging from individual bloggers to SMBs to Fortune 500 companies. Cloudflare protects and accelerates any Internet application online without adding hardware, installing software, or changing a line of code. Internet properties powered by Cloudflare all have web traffic routed through its intelligent global network, which gets smarter with every request. As a result, they see significant improvement in performance and a decrease in spam and other attacks. Cloudflare was named to Entrepreneur Magazine’s Top Company Cultures list and ranked among the World’s Most Innovative Companies by Fast Company.  At Cloudflare, we’re not looking for people who wait for a polished roadmap; we’re looking for the builders who see the cracks in the Internet that everyone else has simply learned to live with. We value candidates who have the instinct to spot a "normalized" problem and the AI-native curiosity to create a solution using the latest tools. Our culture is built on iteration, leveraging AI to ship faster today to make it better tomorrow, while ensuring that every improvement, no matter how small, is shared across the team to lift everyone up. If you’re the type of person who values curiosity over bureaucracy, and that AI is a partner in solving tough problems to keep the Internet moving forward, you’ll fit right in. Available location:  Austin, Texas About the Department The Marketing team at Cloudflare is dedicated to amplifying our mission to help build a better Internet. We craft compelling narratives, engage with our global audience, and drive awareness and adoption of our innovative products and services. Through strategic campaigns, content creation, and community engagement, we play a crucial role in connecting with our customers and the broader internet community. As an intern, you'll join a dynamic and supportive environment, learning from experienced professionals and contributing to impactful projects. About the Role As a Content Marketing Intern at Cloudflare, you will play a vital role in shaping the voice of our brand and engaging our audience across various digital platforms. You will contribute to creating compelling content that attracts potential customers and keeps our current community informed and engaged throughout their journey with Cloudflare. This role offers an exciting opportunity to develop your writing skills, understand digital content strategy, and learn about the intersection of technology, marketing, and global reach. You will be a key contributor to our team, bringing fresh perspectives and a passion for storytelling to our content initiatives. Responsibilities Collaborate with the marketing team to develop and execute content strategies that align with business goals. Research, write, and edit engaging blog posts, newsletters, case studies, and other marketing collateral. Ensure all content reflects Cloudflare's brand voice and maintains a high standard of quality and accuracy. Learn and apply best practices in SEO (Search Engine Optimization) and AEO (AI Engine Optimization) to optimize content for discoverability. Adapt writing style and tone for various digital platforms and target audiences, understanding the nuances of each channel. Contribute to content planning and brainstorming sessions, bringing creative ideas to the table. Support the team in analyzing content performance and identifying opportunities for improvement. Skills & Qualifications Currently pursuing a degree in Journalism, English, Marketing, Communications, or a related field. Available for a full-time 12-week internship, working from our Austin office 3-5 days a week during the summer (May-Aug). A strong portfolio showcasing your writing samples across different formats (e.g., blog posts, articles, essays). Demonstrable understanding of SEO (Search Engine Optimization) and AEO (AI Engine Optimization) principles. Ability to write clearly, concisely, and adaptably for various digital platforms and audiences. Experience with AI editing tools, brand compliance, and productivity. Strong research skills and a keen eye for detail. Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Curiosity and a proactive approach to learning and problem-solving. A passion for technology and its impact on the internet is desirable. You can check out our internship blogs to learn more about our program and hear directly from our past interns. What Makes Cloudflare Special? We’re not just a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company. We’re a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company with a soul. Fundamental to our mission to help build a better Internet is protecting the free and open Internet. Project Galileo : Since 2014, we've equipped more than 2,400 journalism and civil society organizations in 111 countries with powerful tools to defend themselves against attacks that would otherwise censor their work, technology already used by Cloudflare’s enterprise customers--at no cost. Athenian Project : In 2017, we created the Athenian Project to ensure that state and local governments have the highest level of protection and reliability for free, so that their constituents have access to election information and voter registration. Since the project, we've provided services to more than 425 local government election websites in 33 states. 1.1.1.1 : We released 1.1.1.1 to help fix the foundation of the Internet by building a faster, more secure and privacy-centric public DNS resolver. This is available publicly for everyone to use - it is the first consumer-focused service Cloudflare has ever released. Here’s the deal - we don’t store client IP addresses never, ever. We will continue to abide by our privacy commitment and ensure that no user data is sold to advertisers or used to target consumers. Sound like something you’d like to be a part of? We’d love to hear from you! This position may require access to information protected under U.S. export control laws, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations. Please note that any offer of employment may be conditioned on your authorization to receive software or technology controlled under these U.S. export laws without sponsorship for an export license. Cloudflare is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.  We are committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all people and place great value in both diversity and inclusiveness.  All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to their, or any other person's, perceived or actual race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, family care status, or any other basis protected by law. We are an AA/Veterans/Disabled Employer. Cloudflare provides reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities.  Please tell us if you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job. Examples of reasonable accommodations include, but are not limited to, changing the application process, providing documents in an alternate format, using a sign language interpreter, or using specialized equipment.  If you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job, please contact us via e-mail at hr@cloudflare.com or via mail at 101 Townsend St. San Francisco, CA 94107.

jobs.title: Marketing Operations Intern (Summer 2026)
jobs.content_plain: About Us At Cloudflare, we are on a mission to help build a better Internet. Today the company runs one of the world’s largest networks that powers millions of websites and other Internet properties for customers ranging from individual bloggers to SMBs to Fortune 500 companies. Cloudflare protects and accelerates any Internet application online without adding hardware, installing software, or changing a line of code. Internet properties powered by Cloudflare all have web traffic routed through its intelligent global network, which gets smarter with every request. As a result, they see significant improvement in performance and a decrease in spam and other attacks. Cloudflare was named to Entrepreneur Magazine’s Top Company Cultures list and ranked among the World’s Most Innovative Companies by Fast Company.  At Cloudflare, we’re not looking for people who wait for a polished roadmap; we’re looking for the builders who see the cracks in the Internet that everyone else has simply learned to live with. We value candidates who have the instinct to spot a "normalized" problem and the AI-native curiosity to create a solution using the latest tools. Our culture is built on iteration, leveraging AI to ship faster today to make it better tomorrow, while ensuring that every improvement, no matter how small, is shared across the team to lift everyone up. If you’re the type of person who values curiosity over bureaucracy, and that AI is a partner in solving tough problems to keep the Internet moving forward, you’ll fit right in. Available location:  Austin, TX, San Francisco, CA, NYC About the Team The Marketing team at Cloudflare is at the forefront of driving our brand presence, engaging our global audience, and supporting the growth of our business. We are a dynamic group of passionate individuals dedicated to delivering impactful campaigns and innovative strategies. Within Marketing, the Operations function is critical to our success, providing the infrastructure, data, and processes that enable our campaigns to run smoothly and effectively. We empower the entire marketing organization by ensuring efficiency, accuracy, and scalability in everything we do. As an Intern, you'll be joining a team that values curiosity, transparency, and a continuous learning mindset, where your contributions directly support our mission to help build a better Internet. About the Role As a Marketing Operations intern, you will support our marketing technology stack, campaign execution, and data operations. This role sits at the intersection of marketing, data analytics, and marketing technology, helping ensure campaigns run smoothly, data is accurate, and marketing systems are optimized. This is an exciting opportunity for a curious and proactive individual to gain hands-on experience in a fast-paced technology company. You will contribute to the setup, testing, and deployment of marketing campaigns across various digital channels, ensuring seamless execution. Your responsibilities will also include maintaining the integrity and accuracy of our marketing databases and assisting in generating insightful performance reports that inform our strategies.You will have the opportunity to learn about and support the administration of marketing technology platforms, helping to optimize our tools and processes. Organizing digital assets, documenting best practices, and collaborating cross-functionally with various teams will be key aspects of your role, contributing to overall operational efficiency. If you are eager to learn, solve problems, and contribute to a collaborative environment, this internship offers a unique chance to make a tangible impact and develop valuable skills in marketing operations. Responsibilities Assist with the setup, testing, and deployment of marketing campaigns across multiple channels, ensuring accuracy and timely execution. Support the maintenance of marketing databases, focusing on data quality, integrity, and cleanliness. Contribute to the generation of marketing performance reports, helping to track key metrics and provide initial insights. Learn and assist in the administration of various marketing technology platforms (MarTech), ensuring they function effectively. Help organize and manage digital assets, ensuring they are easily accessible and properly categorized. Document marketing operations processes and procedures to enhance team efficiency and knowledge sharing. Collaborate cross-functionally with marketing, sales, and other teams to ensure smooth campaign execution. Participate in various ad-hoc projects, providing support and contributing to team objectives as needed. Skills & Qualifications Currently pursuing a degree (Master’s preferred) in Marketing, Business, Information Systems, or a related field. Available for a full-time 12-week internship, working from one of our US hub offices 3-5 days a week during the summer (May-Aug). Demonstrated analytical mindset with an ability to approach problems systematically. Technically adept and eager to learn new systems and software quickly. Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, for effective cross-functional collaboration. Strong organizational abilities with a keen eye for detail and process. Proactive attitude, eager to take initiative and contribute solutions. A collaborative team player who thrives in a dynamic environment. Basic proficiency in Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets for data manipulation and analysis. Foundational understanding of digital marketing concepts and principles. Bonus Points Familiarity with CRM (e.g., Salesforce),Marketing Automation Platforms (e.g., Marketo), Adobe tools. Experience with project management tools (e.g., Asana, Jira). A passion for technology and a curiosity about how it impacts business operations. Compensation Compensation may be adjusted depending on work location. Estimated hourly pay of $24/hour. You can check out our internship blogs to learn more about our program and hear directly from our past interns. What Makes Cloudflare Special? We’re not just a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company. We’re a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company with a soul. Fundamental to our mission to help build a better Internet is protecting the free and open Internet. Project Galileo : Since 2014, we've equipped more than 2,400 journalism and civil society organizations in 111 countries with powerful tools to defend themselves against attacks that would otherwise censor their work, technology already used by Cloudflare’s enterprise customers--at no cost. Athenian Project : In 2017, we created the Athenian Project to ensure that state and local governments have the highest level of protection and reliability for free, so that their constituents have access to election information and voter registration. Since the project, we've provided services to more than 425 local government election websites in 33 states. 1.1.1.1 : We released 1.1.1.1 to help fix the foundation of the Internet by building a faster, more secure and privacy-centric public DNS resolver. This is available publicly for everyone to use - it is the first consumer-focused service Cloudflare has ever released. Here’s the deal - we don’t store client IP addresses never, ever. We will continue to abide by our privacy commitment and ensure that no user data is sold to advertisers or used to target consumers. Sound like something you’d like to be a part of? We’d love to hear from you! This position may require access to information protected under U.S. export control laws, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations. Please note that any offer of employment may be conditioned on your authorization to receive software or technology controlled under these U.S. export laws without sponsorship for an export license. We are an AA/Veterans/Disabled Employer. Cloudflare provides reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities.  Please tell us if you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job. Examples of reasonable accommodations include, but are not limited to, changing the application process, providing documents in an alternate format, using a sign language interpreter, or using specialized equipment.  If you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job, please contact us via e-mail at hr@cloudflare.com or via mail at 101 Townsend St. San Francisco, CA 94107.

jobs.title: Marketing Events and Campaigns Intern (Summer 2026)
jobs.content_plain: About Us At Cloudflare, we are on a mission to help build a better Internet. Today the company runs one of the world’s largest networks that powers millions of websites and other Internet properties for customers ranging from individual bloggers to SMBs to Fortune 500 companies. Cloudflare protects and accelerates any Internet application online without adding hardware, installing software, or changing a line of code. Internet properties powered by Cloudflare all have web traffic routed through its intelligent global network, which gets smarter with every request. As a result, they see significant improvement in performance and a decrease in spam and other attacks. We value candidates who have the instinct to spot a "normalized" problem and the AI-native curiosity to create a solution using the latest tools. If you’re the type of person who values curiosity over bureaucracy, and that AI is a partner in solving tough problems to keep the Internet moving forward, you’ll fit right in. Available location:  London, UK About the Team The Marketing team at Cloudflare is at the forefront of sharing our mission to help build a better internet. We are a dynamic and fast-paced team dedicated to showcasing Cloudflare's innovative products and solutions to a global audience. Through strategic campaigns, engaging events, and compelling content, we drive awareness, generate demand, and support our company's rapid growth. As an intern, you'll gain hands-on experience and contribute directly to our efforts in making an impact. About the Role As a Marketing Events & Campaigns Intern, you will play a crucial supporting role in the successful execution of Cloudflare's marketing initiatives. This is an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience across a variety of marketing channels, including webinars, trade shows, and digital advertising. You will contribute to the end-to-end process, from logistical coordination to ensuring our creative assets are polished and ready for launch. Your work will directly support lead generation efforts and help deliver impactful marketing experiences to our prospects and customers. Responsibilities Assist with the logistical planning and execution of virtual webinars and in-person trade shows, including scheduling, vendor coordination, and materials preparation. Support the development and deployment of digital ad campaigns across various platforms, ensuring proper tracking and optimization. Coordinate the readiness of marketing assets, such as website banners, email communications, and landing pages, collaborating with design and content teams. Help manage and track leads generated from events and campaigns, ensuring data accuracy and timely follow-up. Contribute to post-event and campaign reporting by gathering data and assisting with performance analysis. Participate in team meetings and contribute ideas for improving marketing processes and campaign effectiveness. Skills & Qualifications Currently pursuing a Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field. Available for a full-time 12-week internship, working from our London office 3-5 days a week during the summer (May-Aug). Strong organizational skills with a keen eye for detail and the ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Familiarity with digital marketing concepts and platforms is a plus. Proactive attitude with a desire to learn and contribute in a fast-paced environment. Ability to collaborate effectively within a team and with cross-functional partners. Proficiency with standard office productivity tools (e.g., Google Workspace, Microsoft Office). Bonus Points Previous experience (academic or professional) with event coordination or digital campaign support. Understanding of lead tracking and CRM systems. You can check out our internship blogs to learn more about our program and hear directly from our past interns. What Makes Cloudflare Special? We’re not just a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company. We’re a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company with a soul. Fundamental to our mission to help build a better Internet is protecting the free and open Internet. Project Galileo : Since 2014, we've equipped more than 2,400 journalism and civil society organizations in 111 countries with powerful tools to defend themselves against attacks that would otherwise censor their work, technology already used by Cloudflare’s enterprise customers--at no cost. Athenian Project : In 2017, we created the Athenian Project to ensure that state and local governments have the highest level of protection and reliability for free, so that their constituents have access to election information and voter registration. Since the project, we've provided services to more than 425 local government election websites in 33 states. 1.1.1.1 : We released 1.1.1.1 to help fix the foundation of the Internet by building a faster, more secure and privacy-centric public DNS resolver. This is available publicly for everyone to use - it is the first consumer-focused service Cloudflare has ever released. Here’s the deal - we don’t store client IP addresses never, ever. We will continue to abide by our privacy commitment and ensure that no user data is sold to advertisers or used to target consumers. Sound like something you’d like to be a part of? We’d love to hear from you! This position may require access to information protected under U.S. export control laws, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations. Please note that any offer of employment may be conditioned on your authorization to receive software or technology controlled under these U.S. export laws without sponsorship for an export license. We’re not just a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company. We’re a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company with a soul. Fundamental to our mission to help build a better Internet is protecting the free and open Internet. Project Galileo : Since 2014, we've equipped more than 2,400 journalism and civil society organizations in 111 countries with powerful tools to defend themselves against attacks that would otherwise censor their work, technology already used by Cloudflare’s enterprise customers--at no cost. Athenian Project : In 2017, we created the Athenian Project to ensure that state and local governments have the highest level of protection and reliability for free, so that their constituents have access to election information and voter registration. Since the project, we've provided services to more than 425 local government election websites in 33 states. 1.1.1.1 : We released 1.1.1.1 to help fix the foundation of the Internet by building a faster, more secure and privacy-centric public DNS resolver. This is available publicly for everyone to use - it is the first consumer-focused service Cloudflare has ever released. Here’s the deal - we don’t store client IP addresses never, ever. We will continue to abide by our privacy commitment and ensure that no user data is sold to advertisers or used to target consumers. Sound like something you’d like to be a part of? We’d love to hear from you! This position may require access to information protected under U.S. export control laws, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations. Please note that any offer of employment may be conditioned on your authorization to receive software or technology controlled under these U.S. export laws without sponsorship for an export license. Cloudflare is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.  We are committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all people and place great value in both diversity and inclusiveness.  All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to their, or any other person's, perceived or actual race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, family care status, or any other basis protected by law. We are an AA/Veterans/Disabled Employer. Cloudflare provides reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities.  Please tell us if you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job. Examples of reasonable accommodations include, but are not limited to, changing the application process, providing documents in an alternate format, using a sign language interpreter, or using specialized equipment.  If you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job, please contact us via e-mail at hr@cloudflare.com or via mail at 101 Townsend St. San Francisco, CA 94107.

jobs.title: Commercial Product MBA Intern, Summer 2026
jobs.content_text:
Waabi, founded by AI visionary Raquel Urtasun, is the leader in Physical AI. With a world-class team, we're unlocking the next era of autonomous transportation with technology that's powering commercial autonomous trucks and robotaxis. Waabi is backed by and partners with world leaders in AI, automotive, logistics, and deep tech.

With offices in Toronto, San Francisco, Dallas, and Pittsburgh, Waabi is growing quickly and looking for diverse, innovative and collaborative candidates who want to impact the world in a positive way. To learn more visit: www.waabi.ai


Application Instructions:
The Summer Internship is expected to start May/June 2026 and continue on a full-time basis (40 hours/week) on-site to August/September 2026.
This role requires a successful candidate to be on-site at our San Francisco office.
 
Waabi’s Commercial Product team defines and delivers the end-to-end commercial product—network, services, and operations—that makes autonomous trucking deployable and scalable. We sit between autonomy and customers, turning driverless capability into something that can be launched, sold, and 
expanded.

We’re hiring an MBA Intern to own and drive a critical segment of Waabi’s autonomous trucking network and services product. You will take ownership of a real, high-impact problem and drive it from ambiguity to initial deployment plan.

Example areas include:
Designing elements of our trucking network (site strategy, facility requirements, operating models).
Defining service offerings that enable driverless operations (e.g., AV maintenance, data workflows, command center).
Structuring early commercial pilots and scaling framework.

You will:
Own a scoped problem end-to-end: problem definition → requirements → tradeoffs → recommended plan.
Build structured frameworks across product, operations, and GTM.
Drive cross-functional alignment (autonomy, operations, commercial).
Make explicit tradeoffs across time, capital, and autonomy readiness.
Deliver a clear, executive-ready recommendation with measurable impact.

Qualifications:
Currently pursuing an MBA degree, graduating December 2026 onwards.
Experience in product, GTM, or operations (AV, logistics, or consulting preferred).
Excellent communication skills to collaborate across technical and business stakeholders.
Proven ability to move from strategy to execution.
Strong analytical and first-principles thinking.
Ability to operate in ambiguity and drive independent ownership.

This is not a traditional PM role building software features. Your role is to construct the product blueprint and commercial processes essential for scaling driverless trucking.
 
The US yearly hourly range for this role is: $31 - $58 USD/hr in addition to competitive perks. Waabi US Inc.’s yearly salary ranges are determined based on several factors in accordance with the Company’s compensation practices. The hourly base range is reflective of the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position across all US locations.
